title: Add rate limiting to auth endpoints
description:
## Issue
No rate limiting on authentication endpoints allows brute-force attacks.

## Requirements
- Add rate limiting middleware for auth endpoints
- Requires new dependency and design work — track separately

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/routes.rs
- crates/wifisync-server/Cargo.toml

title: Make bcrypt cost configurable
description:
## Issue
Bcrypt uses DEFAULT_COST (12), not configurable. Making it configurable allows tuning per deployment.

## Requirements
- Add BCRYPT_COST env var to ServerConfig
- Default to 12, validate range 4-31

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/config.rs
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/handlers/auth.rs:39,70

title: Parallelize independent package builds in build.sh
description:
## Requirements
- Run independent builds concurrently using background processes and wait
- RPM/DEB/Android builds are independent and can run in parallel
- After compile-once change, fan out 4 packaging jobs + Android in parallel
- Properly handle errors from parallel jobs (fail if any child fails)

## Context
- packaging/build.sh build_all() runs everything sequentially
- Docker builds are independent containers
- Android build is independent of Linux packaging

## Acceptance Criteria
- build_all runs packaging jobs in parallel
- Errors from any parallel job cause overall failure
- Output is still collected in dist/

title: Add --parallel flag to Gradle Android build
description:
## Requirements
- Add --parallel flag to Gradle invocation for parallel task execution
- Combine with the single-invocation change

## Context
- packaging/build.sh build_android()
- Gradle --parallel enables concurrent task execution within a single build

## Acceptance Criteria
- Gradle invocation includes --parallel flag
- Build still produces correct debug and release APKs

title: Fix Docker health check to verify HTTP server
description:
## Issue
Current HEALTHCHECK uses 'sqlite3 /data/wifisync.db SELECT 1' which only verifies the database file exists. A hung HTTP server process passes this check.

## Requirements
- Change health check to curl the /health endpoint
- Add curl to the runtime Docker image
- Verify the HTTP server is actually responding

## Files
- Dockerfile:97-98

title: Use Docker BuildKit cache mounts for Cargo directories
description:
## Requirements
- Use BuildKit --mount=type=cache for Cargo registry, git, and target directories
- Apply to both Dockerfile.fedora and Dockerfile.ubuntu
- Requires DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 or Docker Engine 23+

## Context
- packaging/docker/Dockerfile.fedora and Dockerfile.ubuntu
- BuildKit cache mounts are the modern approach for build caching
- Complements volume mounts for runtime docker run caching

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dockerfiles use RUN --mount=type=cache where applicable
- Build scripts set DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 if needed

title: Enforce JWT expiration during token validation
description:
## Issue
Validation::default() in jsonwebtoken crate behavior needs verification. The security review claims it does NOT validate token expiration by default, allowing expired tokens to be decoded before the is_expired() check.

## Requirements
- Verify Validation::default() behavior for exp claim
- If not validated, explicitly set validate_exp = true
- Add test proving expired tokens are rejected at the decode level
- Clean up redundant is_expired() check if decode already handles it

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/auth.rs:63-70
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/auth.rs:106-108

title: Compile once, package twice — build binaries once then fan out to RPM/DEB packaging
description:
## Requirements
- Build both CLI and server binaries in a single cargo build invocation
- Pass pre-built binaries to RPM/DEB packaging containers instead of recompiling
- Restructure entrypoint scripts to accept a pre-built binary path
- Cut 4 Cargo compiles down to 1

## Context
- packaging/build.sh orchestrates all builds
- packaging/docker/rpm-entrypoint.sh and deb-entrypoint.sh run cargo build inside Docker
- packaging/rpm/wifisync.spec and packaging/deb/debian/rules both call cargo build --release
- Same for server variants

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single cargo build produces both wifisync and wifisync-server binaries
- RPM and DEB builds use pre-built binaries, no cargo compilation inside packaging containers
- All 4 packages (cli-rpm, cli-deb, server-rpm, server-deb) still produce correct output

title: Mount Cargo cache volumes in Docker packaging builds
description:
## Requirements
- Mount persistent Docker volumes for ~/.cargo/registry and ~/.cargo/git
- Second+ builds only recompile changed code, not all dependencies
- Add volume mounts to all 4 docker run invocations in build-rpm.sh, build-deb.sh, build-rpm-server.sh, build-deb-server.sh

## Context
- Each Docker run starts with empty Cargo cache
- Every dependency is re-downloaded and re-compiled from scratch
- Named volumes persist between runs

## Acceptance Criteria
- Docker run commands include -v cargo-registry:/root/.cargo/registry -v cargo-git:/root/.cargo/git
- Subsequent builds are significantly faster due to cached dependencies

title: Combine Gradle assembleDebug and assembleRelease into single invocation
description:
## Requirements
- Combine two separate Gradle invocations into one
- Remove --no-daemon flag to allow daemon reuse
- Change from:
  ./gradlew assembleDebug --no-daemon --no-configuration-cache
  ./gradlew assembleRelease --no-daemon --no-configuration-cache
  To:
  ./gradlew assembleDebug assembleRelease --no-configuration-cache

## Context
- packaging/build.sh build_android() lines 227-230
- Each --no-daemon invocation has JVM startup overhead

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single Gradle invocation builds both debug and release APKs
- Both APKs still copied to dist/ correctly

title: Enable SQLite WAL mode for better concurrency
description:
## Issue
SQLite not using WAL mode. WAL improves concurrent read performance for multi-client sync server.

## Requirements
- Enable WAL mode via PRAGMA after database connection
- Add test

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/db/mod.rs:12-15

title: Make CORS configuration restrictive by default
description:
## Issue
CORS allows all origins, methods, and headers. While this server is primarily for mobile/CLI (where CORS is irrelevant), it's good practice to restrict.

## Requirements
- Make CORS origins configurable via CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var
- Restrict allowed methods to GET, POST, DELETE
- Restrict allowed headers to Content-Type and Authorization

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/api/routes.rs:18-21
- crates/wifisync-server/src/config.rs

title: Reject empty JWT_SECRET environment variable
description:
## Issue
If JWT_SECRET="" (empty string) is explicitly set, env::var() succeeds and returns empty string. An empty secret makes JWTs trivially forgeable since anyone can sign tokens with an empty key.

## Requirements
- Treat empty JWT_SECRET same as unset (generate random or reject)
- Add validation that panics/errors on startup if JWT_SECRET is empty in production
- Add test

## Files
- crates/wifisync-server/src/config.rs:31-40
